meta.name: CFS Chalder Fatigue Scale
meta.description: The Chalder Fatigue Scale is an 11-item questionnaire measuring the severity of physical and mental fatigue. It is widely used in clinical and research settings to assess fatigue in various populations.
meta.author: Chalder, Berelowitz, Pawlikowska, Watts, Wessely, Wright & Wallace (1993)
meta.reference: Chalder T, Berelowitz G, Pawlikowska T, Watts L, Wessely S, Wright D, Wallace EP. Development of a fatigue scale. J Psychosom Res. 1993;37(2):147-153.
meta.instruction: We would like to know more about any problems you have had with feeling tired, weak, or lacking in energy in the last month. Please answer ALL the questions by selecting the answer which applies to you most closely.

questions.text: Do you have problems with tiredness?
Do you need to rest more?
Do you feel sleepy or drowsy?
Do you have problems starting things?
Do you have less strength in your muscles?
Do you have problems thinking clearly?
Do you make slips of the tongue when speaking?

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your fatigue levels are within the normal range. No significant fatigue concerns detected.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good sleep hygiene and regular physical activity
Keep a balanced diet and stay hydrated
Monitor your energy levels if you notice changes

ranges.label: Mild Fatigue
ranges.description: You are experiencing mild fatigue that may slightly impact your daily functioning.
suggestions: Ensure you are getting adequate sleep (7-9 hours per night)
Review your daily schedule for opportunities to rest
Consider light exercise to boost energy levels
If fatigue persists, consult a healthcare provider

ranges.label: Moderate Fatigue
ranges.description: You are experiencing moderate fatigue that may be affecting your work, social activities, and overall quality of life.
suggestions: Consult a healthcare provider to rule out medical causes
Prioritize rest and energy management throughout the day
Consider pacing strategies to manage your activities
Review medications and lifestyle factors that may contribute to fatigue

ranges.label: Severe Fatigue
ranges.description: You are experiencing severe fatigue that likely significantly impacts your daily functioning and quality of life.
suggestions: Seek medical evaluation promptly to identify underlying causes
Discuss treatment options with your healthcare provider
Practice strict energy conservation and pacing
Consider psychological support if fatigue is linked to mood or stress

physical.label: Normal Physical Fatigue
physical.description: Your physical fatigue is within the normal range.

physical.label: Mild Physical Fatigue
physical.description: You are experiencing mild physical fatigue. Ensure adequate rest and physical activity.

physical.label: Moderate Physical Fatigue
physical.description: You are experiencing notable physical fatigue. Consider consulting a healthcare provider.

physical.label: Severe Physical Fatigue
physical.description: You are experiencing severe physical fatigue. Medical evaluation is recommended.

mental.label: Normal Mental Fatigue
mental.description: Your mental fatigue is within the normal range.

mental.label: Mild Mental Fatigue
mental.description: You are experiencing mild mental fatigue. Ensure adequate rest and cognitive breaks.

mental.label: Moderate Mental Fatigue
mental.description: You are experiencing notable mental fatigue that may affect concentration and memory.

mental.label: Severe Mental Fatigue
mental.description: You are experiencing severe mental fatigue. Consider seeking professional evaluation.
